question_text: Which circuit diagram correctly shows two light bulbs connected in series with a battery and switch?

options.label: A
options.text: Battery → Switch → Bulb 1 → Bulb 2 → back to Battery (single loop)

options.label: B
options.text: Battery → Switch → Bulb 1, with Bulb 2 branching off in a separate loop

options.label: C
options.text: Two separate circuits: one with Battery → Bulb 1, another with Battery → Bulb 2

options.label: D
options.text: Battery with two bulbs connected directly across it with no switch

explanation: In a series circuit, all components are connected in a single loop, one after the other. The current flows through the switch, then bulb 1, then bulb 2, and back to the battery. This is the only way to have a proper series circuit.
